Title: UKM first in Asean to integrate BlackBerry's QNX programme into curriculum
Article:
BANGI: Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM) has become the first university in Asean to integrate BlackBerry's QNX Everywhere programme and QNX foundational operating system to strengthen Malaysia's software engineering talent pipeline.
The initiative marks a key milestone in a multi-year strategic partnership between UKM and BlackBerry, with the university set to offer QNX Everywhere as a curricular elective beginning in late 2026.
BlackBerry vice-president of QNX engineering and head of research and development for Asia-Pacific Raj Jain said the programme would equip future engineers with practical experience in developing software for safety-critical industries.
"The future of robotics, automotive and industrial systems is being shaped by Physical AI, where artificial intelligence meets real-world, safety-critical environments.
"With QNX Everywhere, we are giving Malaysia's next generation of engineers hands-on experience with the trusted software foundation that powers safe, secure, real-time systems," he said.
Representatives from both organisations, alongside government and industry stakeholders, have begun designing the course framework and opening registration to students.
UKM students in computer science and engineering will receive free access to the QNX Software Development Platform 8.0 under the programme.
This will enable them to work with the same platform used by leading medical device manufacturers and embedded in more than 275 million vehicles worldwide.
The curriculum is designed to prepare students for careers in advanced manufacturing, semiconductors, robotics, automation and other connected, safety-critical sectors.
UKM vice-chancellor Prof Datuk Dr Sufian Jusoh said the partnership would help produce globally competitive software engineers capable of supporting Malaysia's digital economy ambitions.
"Our partnership with BlackBerry continues to break new ground, expanding to address Malaysia's need to develop more software engineers to support our future digital economy.
"UKM is proud to be the first university in Asean to integrate QNX Everywhere into our curriculum, ensuring that Malaysia's future software engineers are not just career-ready, but globally competitive in designing and supporting the world's most critical industries," he said.
The latest initiative follows the launch of the 24-week Cyber Pathways professional certification programme in May.
The UKM-BlackBerry collaboration will expand beyond cybersecurity into software systems used in mission-critical industries.
Malaysia's policy frameworks, including Industry4WRD, the National 4IR Policy, the New Industrial Master Plan 2030 and Malaysia Digital 2030, emphasise accelerating digital transformation and developing high-value industries.
The QNX Everywhere programme is intended to support these goals by equipping students with industry-relevant skills.
#kw: UKM, BlackBerry, QNX Everywhere, software engineering, Industry 5.0, digital economy, Malaysia, nst, news

Title: Trust crisis deepens as AI reshapes PR landscape
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The public relations (PR) industry must navigate a new era of artificial intelligence (AI), misinformation and digital disruption as technology reshapes how organisations communicate and build trust.
More than 300 communications professionals from Malaysia, Asean and other countries gathered at the 8th Kuala Lumpur International PR Conference (KLIP 8) on June 8-9 to examine the growing challenges facing the industry.
The conference, organised by World Communications, brought together representatives from multinational corporations, government ministries, government-linked companies, media organisations, universities and regional PR associations.
The event focused on public trust, misinformation, digital manipulation and the rapid adoption of AI tools.
World Communications managing director Jaffri Amin Osman said the profession is being transformed by AI-powered communications, predictive issue management, real-time crisis response and the rising importance of trust as a business asset.
"KLIP exists to help practitioners navigate this new reality with clarity and confidence.
"As Malaysia and Asean step into an AI-accelerated future, communication leaders must evolve from storytellers into strategic advisors guiding organisations with insight, integrity and purpose," he said.
In a keynote address delivered on behalf of Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching, he said the rapid evolution of AI and digital platforms highlighted the need for stronger governance, greater platform accountability and safeguards to protect communities, especially children.
"Technology is not the problem; the real danger emerges when powerful systems operate without transparency or ethical safeguards.
"This is why the government remains committed to strengthening online safety, improving digital literacy and investing in sovereign AI capabilities that keep Malaysia's interests secure," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Selangor Sultan disappointed by criticism of state flagpole
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The flying of the Jalur Gemilang and the Selangor state flag on towering flagpoles is not merely about creating a physical structure or landmark, but serves as a symbol of patriotism, loyalty and unity that should be cherished, the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, said.
In a post on the Selangor Royal Office's Facebook page, the ruler said such displays could strengthen patriotic values and instil pride in both the state and the nation.
The post said Sultan Sharafuddin, during a visit to Istanbul, Turkiye, expressed admiration for the prominent flagpoles erected on hills across the city, describing them as a clear symbol of the Turkish people's patriotism.
"His Royal Highness is of the view that flags flown on such flagpoles can foster love for the state and country, promote respect for symbols of sovereignty, and strengthen unity among people of different races and religions," the post said.
[[nid:1460699]]
The ruler also expressed disappointment that the Selangor State Flagpole at Dataran Selangor, which was officially inaugurated earlier this year, continued to draw criticism from some quarters.
Sultan Sharafuddin said he hoped the example set by Istanbul would inspire efforts to cultivate patriotism and strengthen unity among Malaysians.
On March 8, the ruler witnessed the raising of the Selangor flag at the 120-metre Dataran Selangor flagpole, which was recognised by the Malaysia Book of Records as the tallest flagpole in Southeast Asia.
The date was chosen to coincide with the birthday of Sultan Sharafuddin's late father, Almarhum Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Alhaj, who played a pivotal role in the development of Shah Alam and its declaration as Selangor's capital in 1978.
The flag-raising ceremony involved a 15m by 30m flag weighing about 50kg and was carried out by 40 Royal Malaysian Navy personnel.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perak flags concerns over Rohingya presence in Manjung
Article:
IPOH: Concerns have emerged over the presence of Rohingya communities in Perak, particularly in Manjung, where some are believed to have established makeshift settlements and informal schooling facilities.
Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said the situation was under close monitoring by the authorities amid concerns over the growing numbers and the authenticity of documents carried by members of the community.
"They are many in Manjung. Most of them are wearing UNHCR badges. We are not sure about the authenticity because the numbers are too big, but it is under our monitoring," he told reporters after launching Perak Astronomy Month (Bulan Falak) at a hotel here today.
Saarani said the matter had also been raised in National Security Council (NSC) discussions, alongside concerns voiced by local residents.
"I take into account that in the NSC meeting, we were informed about this matter as well as the concerns among local residents," he said.
When asked whether the situation had caused problems on the ground, he said large groups tended to develop a sense of confidence due to their numbers.
"They are many, so they feel strong," he added.
Saarani said there was information indicating the existence of makeshift homes and informal schooling within the community.
"Based on my information, some have built houses, although not properly constructed, and there are also schools.
"That is why we need to look at it from many aspects, including security aspects for local residents," he said.
He said enforcement agencies were already monitoring the situation and it remained under observation for any necessary action.
"Yes, it is already on our radar," he added.
When asked about reports that an informal school structure might be demolished, he said verification was needed.
"We need to check it first," he added.
Earlier, citing UNHCR data as of April, Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said Malaysia had 219,472 registered UNHCR cardholders, of whom 197,591 were Myanmar nationals, including 128,200 Rohingya.
He said the refugee issue required a comprehensive,
multi-agency approach
involving countries across the region and could not be resolved through police enforcement alone.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Peugeot engulfed in flames on Federal Highway
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Peugeot was almost completely destroyed in a fire along the Federal Highway near the Mercedes-Benz showroom, heading from Kuala Lumpur towards Shah Alam, today.
The Selangor Fire and Rescue Department said it received a distress call at 1.15pm and dispatched a team from the Petaling Jaya Fire and Rescue Station to the scene.
"The fire engine arrived at 1.28pm and found the Peugeot engulfed in flames.
"The vehicle was estimated to have sustained 90 per cent damage. No casualties were reported," it said in a statement today.
Five firefighters and one fire engine were deployed in the operation.
The cause of the fire is under investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ToYm ecosystem brands earn Asean Records recognition via online-to-offline retail campaign
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Three brands under the Thousands Of Years Media (ToYm) ecosystem have achieved Asean Records recognition through an integrated online-to-offline retail conversion campaign.
The achievement by ToYm Micro Drama Ads, ToYm Wardrobe and Dong Lin Fish Scale Jelly was recorded during the opening of the first ToYm Wardrobe physical store in Bukit Jalil recently.
The ecosystem brands achieved the Asean Records title of "Most Fish Scale Collagen Jelly Tasting Participants at a Single Retail Outlet Opening Event Through Integrated Voucher Conversion Campaigns from Online and Offline Channels".
The campaign recorded 384 verified participants during the launch, with 52 per cent generated through online conversion channels, including ToYm Micro Drama Ads, short-form video content and livestreaming initiatives.
This reflects the strength of the ToYm ecosystem in transforming digital storytelling, short-form content, livestream engagement and voucher-based campaigns into measurable offline retail participation.
Within the campaign, ToYm Micro Drama Ads served as the digital traffic generation and conversion engine, leveraging short videos, livestreaming and digital content to drive participation.
ToYm Wardrobe provided the physical retail venue through its flagship store opening, while Dong Lin Fish Scale Jelly was the featured product at the centre of the tasting activity.
ToYm founder Datuk Jment Lim said the launch marked the company's expansion from digital engagement into physical brand experiences.
"The launch of ToYm Wardrobe is more than just opening a retail outlet. It reflects our vision of turning storytelling, creativity and audience engagement into long-term brand experiences that people can connect with both online and offline," he said.
The achievement reflects the expansion of the ToYm ecosystem from micro dramas, films, livestreaming and digital platforms into physical retail, fashion, beauty, wellness and lifestyle brand experiences.
ToYm, ToYm Wardrobe and ToYm Beaute have collectively received seven Malaysia Book of Records titles across micro drama productions, digital content initiatives, retail activations and consumer engagement campaigns.
The opening also featured delegates from Miss Chinese World Malaysia 2026, who took part in content creation activities, testimonial recordings and brand engagement experiences.
The ToYm Wardrobe concept was developed during the production of the company's first micro drama, "In Character", where opportunities were identified to develop original wardrobe concepts and fashion pieces aligned with character styling and storytelling.
ToYm is an integrated entertainment and media company guided by its four core pillars of Cultural Entertainment, Education, Technology and Marketing.
The company is currently testing its own Bite Shorts micro drama platform alongside the Tix365 ticketing system.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: High Court upholds conviction of 2 men for beating dog, reduces fine to RM30,000 each
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Penang High Court has upheld the conviction of two men for beating a dog three years ago but reduced the fine imposed on each of them from RM50,000 to RM30,000.
Justice Choong Yeow Choy dismissed Rosli Mat Zin and Suhaili Haron's appeal against their conviction under Section 29 (1) of the Animal Welfare Act 2015, finding that the conviction was grounded on admissible direct evidence and properly admitted video evidence.
However, he allowed their appeal against the sentence and varied the RM50,000 fine in default 12 months' imprisonment imposed by the Sessions Court with a fine of RM30,000 each, in default six months' imprisonment.
In his judgment dated June 6, Justice Choong said a clear and unequivocal message must be conveyed to the public that Section 29(1) of the Animal Welfare Act 2015 should be taken seriously, as the provision serves not only a punitive function but also an important deterrent and educative purpose in the public interest.
"This court is of the view that the appropriate sentence in the present case is a fine. Accordingly, a fine of RM30,000 is imposed on each appellant, and in default of payment, six months' imprisonment," he said.
Section 29 (1) of the Act provides that upon conviction, a person is liable to a fine of not less than RM20,000 and not more than RM100,000, or imprisonment for a term not exceeding three years, or both.
According to the judgment, Rosli, 56, a fisherman and Suhaili, 52, a bus driver, were convicted by the Sessions Court for beating a dog after a full trial concluded on July 31, last year.
They were found guilty of beating a dog in Kampung Teluk Tempoyak, Batu Maung, Penang, at 8.30pm on July 8, 2023.
Justice Choong rejected the appellants' argument that the trial judge had erred in fact and law by failing to appreciate that the police investigation was conducted contrary to the provisions and procedures of the Animal Welfare Act.
The appellants contended that under that Act, only appointed animal welfare officers have the statutory authority to investigate offences under the Act.
However, Justice Choong held that while the Act contains provisions relating to animal welfare officers, those provisions do not exclude the general investigative powers of the police, which derive from the Criminal Procedure Code and the Police Act 1967.
He said that no provision in the Animal Welfare Act restricted the police from handling cases arising under the Act.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: English teacher claims trial to sexually assaulting 9-year-old pupil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: An English teacher pleaded not guilty at the Ampang Sessions Court today to charges of sexually assaulting his 9-year-old pupil twice last month.
Mohd Saufi Jama'in,45, was accused of committing the offences against the child, with whom he was in a position of trust as her teacher, with sexual intent on two separate occasions.
The alleged offences took place at the teachers' dining area of the school canteen at 9am on May 5 and 7.
The charges were framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act which provides a maximum 20-year jail term and whipping upon conviction.
The charges were read together with Section 16 of the same Act, relating to offences committed in a relationship of trust as a teacher, which carries an additional jail term of up to five years and a minimum two strokes of the cane.
Saufi entered his plea before judge Ho Kwong Chin.
Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Hairuliqram Hairuddin requested bail of RM20,000 with one surety for both charges.
He also sought additional conditions requiring the accused to report to a police station monthly and prohibiting him from contacting or interfering with the victim and prosecution witnesses.
In the bail application, defence lawyer Francis Pereira sought a RM4,000 bail for each charge on grounds that his client has no prior criminal records.
"He has been a teacher for close to 20 years and has never faced any disciplinary action.
"He cooperated with police throughout the investigation and poses no flight risk," he said.
After hearing both parties, Ho granted bail of RM12,000 with one surety for both charges and imposed the additional conditions sought by the prosecution.
The court fixed Aug 4 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Evicted tenant jailed after failing to pay RM4,300 over slashed car tyres
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A restaurant worker who slashed all four tyres of a woman's car after being evicted from a rented room was fined RM4,000 by the magistrate's court here today.
Magistrate Arun Noval Dass also ordered M. Yuvarajana, 36, to serve five months in jail if he failed to pay the fine after he pleaded guilty to a charge of committing mischief.
The court further ordered the father of one to pay RM300 in compensation to the victim, or serve one month's imprisonment in default.
He failed to pay both amounts and was sent to Kluang Prison to serve his sentence.
Yuvarajana was charged with slashing all four tyres of a car belonging to a 44-year-old woman, causing losses amounting to RM308.
The offence was committed at a supermarket car park in Parit Raja near here at 9.57am on June 6.
The charge was framed under Section 427 of the Penal Code.
According to the facts of the case, the victim returned to her vehicle at about 11am after shopping and discovered that all four tyres had been punctured.
She then sought assistance from supermarket staff to review closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage, which showed the accused slashing the tyres.
The victim subsequently lodged a police report.
Acting on information received, police later arrested the accused in front of a coconut processing premises.
Investigations revealed that the accused acted out of anger after the victim evicted him from a rented room for failing to pay rent.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was represented by lawyer Tan Song Yan of the National Legal Aid Foundation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AirAsia X postpones Bahrain flight route due to regional conflict
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian low-cost airline AirAsia X said on Thursday it has postponed the launch of its planned service via Bahrain until at least August due to the conflict in the Middle East.
AirAsia initially planned to commence the route in June, linking Kuala Lumpur to Bahrain and onward to London's Gatwick airport.
It said in a statement it now expects the service to start in August or September, subject to market conditions.
The airline said affected passengers would be offered refunds or rescheduled bookings.
The budget carrier said it remains committed to opening a hub in Bahrain but needed to take a "measured approach".
"Bahrain continues to play an important role in our long-term growth plans and regional connectivity strategy, and we remain focused on launching services to both Bahrain and London Gatwick when the operating environment is better aligned with our operational and commercial objectives," AirAsia General Manager Benyamin Ismail said.
AirAsia has been hard hit by volatile jet fuel prices, posting a quarterly net loss earlier this year as it was forced to cut 10 per cent of flights and introduce a surcharge of about 20 per cent on fuel.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Discipline teacher allegedly attacked after cutting student's hair
Article:
SERI ISKANDAR:  A discipline teacher is believed to have been injured after allegedly being punched by a group of students at a secondary school in Kuala Kangsar yesterday.
Perak Education, Higher Education, Youth and Sports Committee chairman Datuk Khairuddin Abu Hanipah said he was awaiting a detailed report from the Perak Education Department before issuing any statement.
"I have not received confirmed information and am still waiting for a report from the State Education Department regarding the incident.
"Further checks are being carried out to determine the authenticity of the case before any official statement is issued.
"If the incident did occur, it is an unacceptable act as teachers are educators who guide and nurture our children," he said.
He told reporters this after officiating the Tabika Kemas Gen Alpha Haj Simulation Educational Excellence Programme at Masjid Sultan Yusuff Izzudin Shah in Seri Iskandar today.
It was reported that the incident occurred at 11.30am and is believed to have stemmed from dissatisfaction after the student's hair was cut by the discipline teacher.
The teacher is said to have sustained injuries after being punched by the students.
Khairuddin said the state government would allow the relevant authorities to conduct a thorough investigation before any conclusions are made.
At the event, he said about 400 children from 30 Tabika Kemas centres in Perak Tengah took part in a one-day haj simulation programme organised in collaboration with Andalusia Travel & Tours Sdn Bhd.
He said the programme aimed to expose children early to the basics of the haj pilgrimage and instil understanding of the fifth pillar of Islam.
"It is also a way to encourage children to start saving and nurturing the aspiration of performing the haj when they are capable in the future," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke vows firm action against RTD enforcement officers involved in bribery
Article:
NILAI: The Road Transport Department (RTD) will take firm action against any enforcement officer guilty of bribery, says Transport Minister Anthony Loke.
He said the department maintains a strict zero-tolerance stance on corruption and will not compromise on any proven misconduct involving its personnel.
Loke also urged members of the public with evidence of wrongdoing to channel it directly to the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) for further action.
"We do not compromise on this matter as RTD is committed to a zero-tolerance approach towards corruption," he said.
"If there is evidence from the public captured through video showing attempts to solicit bribes, regardless of who it is or what rank, report it to the MACC," he told reporters after officiating the RTD Nilai branch office here today
He was commenting on allegations that an e-hailing driver was forced to pay RM800 to RTD officers over an offence involving bookings made on behalf of a third party, which went viral on social media yesterday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New RM140mil RTD complex in Negri Sembilan next year – Loke
Article:
NILAI: A new RM140 million Road Transport Department (RTD) complex will be built in Negri Sembilan, with construction set to begin next year.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said it will be developed on the existing site in Senawang under Rolling Plan 1 of the 13th Malaysia Plan. The current building will be demolished to make way for the project.
"The project has been approved under the 13th Malaysia Plan and will be implemented by the Public Works Department (PWD)," he said.
"Construction on the new RM140 million complex will begin after operations at the current building are relocated to a temporary office at Era Walk, Terminal 2 Seremban, early next year.
"The existing RTD building in Senawang will be demolished to make way for the new complex on the same site," he told reporters after officiating the RTD Nilai branch office here today.
Also present were Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah and RTD director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli.
Loke said the design of the new complex will take into account current needs and will be presented once finalised.
He said the immediate focus is to complete the temporary RTD office at Era Walk, which will utilise an existing building in the area.
"By using an existing building as a temporary RTD state office, it will help revitalise the area, which has appeared relatively inactive in recent years.
"We found the location suitable, with ample parking facilities, making it convenient for the public while awaiting completion of the new complex," he said.
On the RTD Nilai branch, Loke said the project involved upgrading an existing building at a cost of RM1.7 million as part of the government's strategic investment in strengthening public service infrastructure at district level.
He said the fourth RTD branch after Kuala Pilah, Jempol and the Ampangan UTC in Seremban is located on the ground floor of Block C, Seremban City Council (MBS) Nilai branch building, making it a strategic and accessible location for the local community.
The location not only provides convenience to the people, but also supports local economic growth, given Nilai's rapid development as an education, industrial, logistics and community hub, he said.
"Construction began on Dec 17, 2025, with continuous monitoring by the ministry and RTD, and operations commenced fully on June 8 this year.
"Adherence to the timeline reflects efficient governance and close cooperation among all parties involved.
"The opening of the RTD Nilai branch is part of efforts to upgrade RTD offices with a more modern and customer-friendly concept," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's SD Guthrie working with Indonesia on palm oil plantation seizures, CEO says
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's SD Guthrie is working with the Indonesian government on the issue of land seizuresaffecting some of its palm oil plantations, its chief executive Mohd Haris Mohd Arshad said on Thursday.
Indonesia launched a task force last year consisting of the military, the police and state prosecutors to crack down on violations in the palm oil industry.
The task force seized around 4.1 million hectares of plantations last year for operating illegally in forest areas, a move that affected both major palm oil companies and smallholder farmers.
SD Guthrie is one of the world's largest palm oil producers and about a third of its total land bank is located in Indonesia. The company has said that 2% of its land has been seized by the task force, amounting to around 2,800 hectares.
The company has not been issued any decree from the Indonesian government related to its plantations, and is still harvesting as usual in the country, Mohd Haris told a press conference.
He also said Indonesia's plan to bring its top commodities exports under a the control of a state agency was understandable as the country aimed to curb revenue leakages.
SD Guthrie was not implicated in such concerns and remains one of the largest taxpayers in Indonesia, Mohd Haris said.
He said the company was not too worried by high fertiliser costs arising from a supply crunch caused by the Middle East conflict, as it procures its own supplies from China, Canada, Belarus, and Egypt. The company would also improve water management to cope with any potential dry weather spells due to El Nino weather conditions this year, Mohd Haris added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Unemployment rate back at 3pct in April
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's unemployment rate returned to 3.0 per cent in April, with 511,800 unemployed persons recorded, according to the Statistics Department.
The department said the unemployment rate rose 0.1 percentage point in April 2026, up from 2.9 per cent in March, returning to the level last seen in October 2025.
"In the meantime, the number of employed persons continued to increase in April 2026, rising by 0.1 per cent to 16.82 million persons against the previous month (March 2026: 16.80 million persons).
"Likewise, the number of unemployed persons remained on an upward trajectory, edging up by 0.6 per cent to 511.8 thousand persons, compared to 509.0 thousand persons in the prior month," it said.
Chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said actively unemployed individuals, defined as those available for work and actively looking for jobs, made up 79.5 per cent of the total unemployed population.
He said the number of actively unemployed persons increased by 0.3 per cent to 407,100 in April 2026 from 405,800 in the previous month.
"A total of 63.9 per cent of the actively unemployed were unemployed for less than three months, while 5.0 per cent were in long-term unemployment for more than a year.
"At the same time, the inactively unemployed, comprising those who believed that no jobs were available, went up by 1.5 per cent to 104.7 thousand persons compared with 103.2 thousand persons in the previous month," Uzir said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Siblings arrested over viral nasi bajet stall theft in Ipoh
Article:
IPOH: Police have arrested two siblings suspected of stealing a cash box and a mobile phone from a nasi bajet trader at Jalan Lebuh Meru Raya here last month.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Muhammad Najib Hamzah said the suspects, aged 24 and 36, were detained in Teluk Intan on Monday following investigations into the incident, which recently went viral on social media.
He said the theft occurred at noon on May 21 while the victim was operating her stall.
"The victim was approached by two men on a motorcycle who intended to purchase food.
"While she was busy packing nasi lemak, one of the suspects took a cash box containing cash and a mobile phone before both fled the scene," he said in a statement today.
Najib said both suspects had admitted their involvement in the theft.
"Investigations also found that they are linked to several other criminal cases in the district," he said.
He said the suspects had been remanded until tomorrow to facilitate investigations and that the case will be referred to the deputy public prosecutor for further action.
The case is being investigated under Section 379 of the Penal Code for theft, which carries a jail term of up to seven years, a fine, or both, upon conviction.
Najib also thanked members of the public whose information and cooperation led to the arrests.
He advised the public to remain vigilant and keep valuables in safe places to avoid becoming victims of crime.
It was previously reported that a roadside nasi bajet stall along Lebuh Meru Raya was
targeted
by two men who allegedly posed as customers before making off with a cash box and a mobile phone.
The 21-year-old trader was serving customers and packing food orders when the suspects, believed to be riding a silver Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycle, stopped at the stall.
While she was packing rice, they allegedly stole a cash box containing cash and her iPhone 13 before fleeing towards the Meru Raya roundabout.
The victim suffered losses estimated at RM2,000.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] MACC detains 73 nationwide over alleged RM9mil Daya Kerjaya claims fraud
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has remanded 73 people, including agents and company owners, over alleged fraudulent claims totalling about RM9 million under the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 employment incentive programme.
Sources said the suspects, comprising 48 men and 25 women aged between their 20s and 70s, were detained on Tuesday at MACC offices nationwide, except in Perlis.
Initial investigations found the offences were allegedly committed between 2024 and 2025.
The suspects are believed to have submitted false claims for government wage incentives of RM1,500 per month for six months for each employee hired by private sector employers and government-linked companies (GLCs).
On June 9, the MACC, through its Intelligence Division, Investigation Division and state offices, carried out a nationwide integrated operation in collaboration with the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso).
The operation involved raids at 90 locations nationwide, including company premises, resulting in the arrest of 83 people comprising six agents and 77 company owners.
Sources said intelligence gathered since November last year had identified 143 company premises and 320 people allegedly linked to the fraudulent claims.
During the operation, MACC officers recorded 104 statements involving 32 suspects and 72 witnesses.
Authorities also froze 25 company bank accounts containing about RM390,000 as part of the investigation.
MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman confirmed the arrests and said the case was being investigated under Section 18 of the MACC Act2009 for submitting false claims.
He said investigations were ongoing and further developments would be announced in due course.
It was previously reported that four suspects, including a woman, were
remanded
for seven days over similar false claims involving Perkeso's Daya Kerjaya employment incentives.
Magistrate Arun Noval Dass allowed the remand application against the suspects, aged between 40 and 60, to facilitate investigations under Section 18 of the MACC Act.
The suspects allegedly posed as workers of a contracting company and submitted claims totalling between RM160,000 and RM180,000.
Perkeso's Daya Kerjaya initiative provides training and job placement assistance to help unemployed individuals improve their skills and secure employment.
Under its conditions, eligible recipients include senior citizens aged 60 and above, persons with disabilities (PwD), and people who have worked at a company for at least six months.
Suspicion over the claims led to the MACC probe and subsequent arrests.
On June 9, Abd Halim said the commission had detected misappropriation involving the initiative, with
false claims
amounting to RM9.01 million.
He said investigations identified 143 companies involving 320 workers nationwide implicated in the misappropriation of government funds managed by Perkeso since 2024.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three friends, including teenager, charged with gang-raping 13-year-old girl
Article:
KOTA BARU: Three friends, including an 18-year-old teenager, were charged in the Sessions Court here today with raping a 13-year-old girl in Kuala Krai last month.
The 18-year-old teenager, together with Mohamad Aiman Firdaus Nazri, 24, and Muhammad Hakimi Razlan, 23, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read separately before judge Ahmad Bazli Bahruddin.
The teenager allegedly committed the offence in a bushed area by the roadside in Kampung Ragu, Manek Urai, at 10.30pm on May 27.
Mohamad Aiman and Muhammad Hakimi allegedly committed the same offence at an abandoned house in Kampung Sungai Tias, Laloh, between 1am and 1.30am on May 28.
The charges were framed under Section 376 of the Penal Code, which carries a jail term of up to 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
Mohamad Aiman and Muhammad Hakimi were also charged with physical contact against the same victim at the same location, time, and date under Section 14 (d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
Deputy public prosecutor Nur Kamilah Che Ibrahim prosecuted, while the 18-year-old was represented by lawyer Muhammad Yusoff Abdullah. The other two accused were represented by lawyer Muhammad Izzat Hasizan.
The court allowed the 18-year-old bail of RM8,000 with one surety and ordered him not to harass witnesses or the victim, alongside reporting monthly to the nearest police station.
The other two accused were granted bail at RM12,000 each with one surety under the same additional conditions.
The court fixed July 12 for case mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man pleads not guilty to drink-driving charge, granted RM12,000 bail
Article:
BATU PAHAT: An eatery assistant pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court here today to a charge of driving under the influence of alcohol last month.
Sor Wee Jie, 32, made the plea after the charge was read to him before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass.
The bachelor was accused of driving while intoxicated, with a blood alcohol level of 192mg/100ml, which exceeded the permitted limit of 50mg/100ml.
The offence was committed at Km6 of Jalan Kluang-Batu Pahat here at 2.05am on May 2.
The charge was framed under Section 45A (1) of the Road Transport Act which carries a maximum fine of RM30,000, up to two years' jail, and disqualification from holding a driving licence upon conviction.
Based on the charge-sheet, police stopped the accused at a roadblock after noticing him driving a car in a suspicious manner.
After identifying themselves, police conducted a breathalyser test and found the accused was driving under the influence of alcohol.
He was then arrested and taken to the district police headquarters.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan prosecuted, while Sor was represented by lawyer Tan Song Yan from the National Legal Aid Foundation.
Following the non-guilty plea, the court allowed bail at RM12,000 with one surety. The accused paid the bail.
The court fixed June 19 for mention and document submission.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Car salesman fined, jailed, for having nude videos of 9-year-old boy
Article:
MUAR:   26-year-old car salesman was sentenced to six months' jail and fined RM3,000 by the magistrate's court here today for possessing child sexual abuse material (CSAM).
Magistrate Kartini Kasran meted out the sentence against Tan Yean Xuen, who pleaded guilty to possessing nude photos and videos of a 9-year-old boy.
The court ordered Tan to serve an additional six months in prison if he failed to pay the fine. He paid the fine.
The bachelor was found with the material on his mobile phone at the Muar district police headquarters Criminal Investigation Department investigating officer's office at 6pm on June 7.
The charge was framed under Section 10 of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
The court heard that the boy's 49-year-old father discovered his son had sent nude photos and videos to an unknown man after checking the child's mobile phone.
Fearing the images would be misused, the father lodged a police report.
At 1.30pm the following day, police arrested Tan in Bandar Puteri, Klang, Selangor, before bringing him to the Muar police headquarters.
An inspection of Tan's mobile phone later revealed several photos and videos of the child.
Deputy public prosecutor Yasmin Mohd Piah prosecuted, while Tan was represented by lawyer Fatimah Zahra Azmi from the National Legal Aid Foundation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man fined RM100 for swearing at cop
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 25-year-old restaurant assistant was fined the maximum of RM100 by the magistrate's court today after admitting to hurling vulgarities at a police corporal during a traffic enforcement operation in Sentul on Monday.
Magistrate Nurul Izzah Hasan Basri ordered Muhammad Syarifuddin Abd Latif to serve a day in jail if he fails to pay the fine.
Syarifuddin pleaded guilty to using insulting words intended to provoke a breach of peace against a 27-year-old policeman attached to the motorcycle patrol unit of Sentul district police headquarters.
The offence was committed at 8.24am along Jalan Dato Senu 3 in Sentul on June 8 this year.
He was charged under Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act which provides a maximum RM100 fine upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Nurainizatul Farhana Zainal urged the court to impose an appropriate sentence as a deterrent and lesson to the accused.
The National Legal Aid Foundation, representing the accused, appealed for a minimal fine on grounds that the accused earns RM2,000 monthly, supporting his parents and is remorseful.
Sentul police chief Assistant Commissioner Basri Sagoni had said a man was arrested for allegedly
hurling insults
at one of his men and obstructing a civil servant from conducting his duties following a traffic enforcement operation in Sentul.
He said the vehicle owner allegedly approached the officer, recorded a video using his mobile phone and uttered abusive remarks while provoking the policeman out of dissatisfaction with the summons issued over a traffic offence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan govt provides rental aid to Kampung Cina fire victims
Article:
GUA MUSANG: The Kelantan government has agreed to cover house rental costs for three months for 27 families affected by the recent fire at Kampung Cina in Kota Baru.
Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud said the aid was intended to ease the burden on victims who lost their homes and belongings in the blaze.
Under the initiative, each affected family will receive RM500 a month for rental expenses, effective immediately.
"Victims are free to rent houses at locations of their choice, and the assistance will be provided for the first three months.
"They need to coordinate with the Kota Baru Land and District Office, which will oversee and manage the implementation of the aid," he told reporters here today.
Nassuruddin said the state government understood the difficulties faced by the victims and remained committed to safeguarding their welfare.
He said the rental assistance was part of the state's efforts to help affected families secure safe and comfortable temporary accommodation while rebuilding their lives.
"The state government will continue to consider and provide various forms of assistance from time to time, based on the needs of the victims and available resources," he added.
Nassuruddin also expressed appreciation to government agencies, uniformed bodies, non-governmental organisations, corporate entities and members of the public for their assistance to the victims.
On June 8, a
fire
destroyed 27 houses in Kampung Cina, leaving more than 100 people homeless.
The victims are currently staying at a temporary relief centre at Masjid Banggol.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PAC finalises report on airport management after KLIA visit
Article:
SEPANG: The Public Accounts Committee (PAC) is in the final stages of completing its report on the management of public airports.
PAC chairman Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said a working visit to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) today would help the committee finalise a report that is "more transparent, comprehensive and thorough" following its inquiry into airport operations and infrastructure projects.
She said the committee would table its report during the upcoming Dewan Rakyat sitting, which begins on June 22.
She said the visit to KLIA formed part of the committee's ongoing inquiry into airport management involving the Finance Ministry, Transport Ministry and Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB).
"The committee is now in the final phase of finalising the draft report on airport management.
"This working visit has helped us prepare a report that is more transparent, comprehensive and thorough," she told reporters after the visit.
Mas Ermieyati said PAC had conducted 19 proceedings on the issue, and the site visit allowed members to verify information and updates presented during previous hearings.
The committee was briefed by Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB), management on the direction, status and progress of several high-impact projects, including the Aerotrain replacement programme and the Baggage Handling System (BHS) upgrade.
She said PAC viewed seriously recent service disruptions at KLIA, which had affected service delivery and Malaysia's image among tourists and foreign investors.
[[nid:1460639]]
"The purpose of today's visit was to assess the current operational performance at KLIA and critically evaluate the progress of transformation plans and priority asset development projects.
"We wanted to see for ourselves the actual progress on the ground and gain a clearer understanding of the issues under investigation," she said.
Mas Ermieyati said PAC had observed encouraging developments involving the Aerotrain service, which she said had been operating around the clock since June 1.
She recalled that several PAC members, including herself, experienced an aerotrain breakdown on July 2 last year, a day after the service was launched by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"Improvements have since been made and we were informed that the Aerotrain is now operating 24 hours a day.
"This is a positive development and I would like to congratulate the relevant parties for their efforts," she said.
On the BHS upgrade, Mas Ermieyati said the committee gained a better appreciation of the project's complexity after inspecting the system firsthand.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pasir Gudang chemical leak sends yellow smoke into air
Article:
PASIR GUDANG: Yellow smoke filled the air after a tank containing spent inorganic acid leaked at an industrial facility here today.
No casualties were reported after the 200-litre tank containing the chemical at an industrial waste management facility leaked this morning.
Spent inorganic acid is a hazardous industrial waste derived from strong acids such as sulphuric or hydrochloric mixtures. Exposure can cause severe chemical burns and respiratory irritation.
It is regulated as scheduled waste due to its corrosive and toxic nature, requiring specialised handling, containment and treatment before disposal.
The leak sparked concern among residents in the vicinity of the industrial park, who took to social media platforms fearing toxic exposure.
Johor Department of Environment (DoE) director Mohd Famey Yusoff said officers were deployed to the scene to conduct ground and air-index assessments.
"More information will be released once the assessments are concluded," he said when asked about the environmental impact.
Pasir Gudang Fire and Rescue operation commander Firdaus Ahmad said firefighters arrived at the scene nine minutes after being alerted.
He said the situation was brought under control by 12.45pm, and the chemical was contained using the facility's scrubber system, which captures and neutralises hazardous fumes.
Operations are ongoing, with the Hazardous Materials (Hazmat) unit securing the area as investigations continue into the cause of the leak, he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor grandfather child abuse case: Nancy says ministry ready to assist victim's family
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Women, Family and Community Development Ministry will leave investigations into the alleged sexual abuse of a 13-year-old girl by her grandfather in Johor to the relevant authorities.
However, Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the ministry stands ready to assist the victim's family, particularly in providing counselling services.
"As it is already a criminal case, we leave it to the authorities concerned.
"We have our counselling team that will contact the family to see whether they wish to proceed or require our assistance in terms of counselling.
"We need to support them, but it is also up to the family, as some may prefer privacy.
"However, we will also take the necessary steps to obtain reports on the case," she said.
Nancy said the ministry would continue to follow its standard operating procedures, as such cases require the involvement of its protection officers.
She was speaking to reporters after officiating at the opening of the Tribunal for Anti-Sexual Harassment (TAGS) office and the launch of the e-TAGS system here today.
Yesterday, a 67-year-old pensioner
was remanded for six days
to assist investigations into allegations that he had repeatedly sexually abused his granddaughter over the past year.
The case drew widespread public attention after a video of the
man's alleged confession
went viral on social media.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Multi-agency approach needed to address Rohingya issue, says Ayob Khan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Rohingya refugee issue requires a comprehensive, multi-agency approach involving countries across the region and cannot be resolved through police enforcement alone.
Citing United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) data as of April, Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said Malaysia has 219,472 registered UNHCR cardholders, of whom 197,591 are Myanmar nationals, including 128,200 Rohingya.
"Of the total, 197,591 were Myanmar nationals, with 128,200 from the Rohingya community," he said.
"The majority of UNHCR cardholders in Malaysia are from Myanmar, and most are Rohingya. Addressing this issue requires the involvement of all parties. The police are the final link in the chain," he said.
He noted that enforcement begins at maritime borders and continues after migrants enter the country, but repatriation remains a challenge.
"However, when they are caught and sentenced, the question arises as to where they should be sent back. That is why this issue requires the cooperation of all parties, including the source country and other related nations," he said.
Speaking at a Police Day community outreach programme at the Tuanku Ampuan Besar Tuanku Aishah Rohani Hospital, UKM Children's Specialist Hospital, he also addressed concerns over Rohingya settlements highlighted on social media.
He said enforcement operations against illegal immigrants and unlawful settlements are carried out continuously and not only when issues attract public attention.
"Operations are ongoing. When an issue goes viral, the public simply becomes more aware of the existence of these settlements," he said.
As an example, he cited a recent operation at Flat Rancangan Sungai Tekali in Hulu Langat, where 42 individuals were detained.
On proposals to establish a Rohingya transit or settlement centre on an island near the Andaman Sea, Ayob Khan said several issues must first be resolved, including ownership, administration, funding and the refugees' eventual destination.
"If a transit centre is established, who will manage and fund it? After screening, where will the refugees be sent? We also need to know whether third countries are prepared to accept them," he said.
He warned that such a facility could become a pull factor for migrant smuggling syndicates.
"Syndicates may exploit the situation by claiming there is already a transit point before refugees are sent to a third country. This could encourage more migrants to enter the region," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tun Sharifah Rodziah sea base decommissioned after a decade in Semporna waters
Article:
SEMPORNA: The Tun Sharifah Rodziah Sea Base (PL TSR), located in the waters off Semporna, was officially decommissioned yesterday after serving for a decade since July 31, 2015 as a forward operating base safeguarding security and sovereignty in the country's waters off eastern Sabah.
The Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) Region 2 Naval Headquarters said in a statement that the full ceremonial decommissioning was led by Eastern Fleet Commander Vice Admiral Datuk Khir Junaidi Idris, marking a symbolic end to the base's service.
The statement said that throughout its decade of service, PL TSR had served as a strategic platform supporting maritime security operations, monitoring areas of maritime interest and strengthening the presence of security forces in the Eastern Sabah Security Zone (ESS Zone).
It said the base's strategic location enabled rapid responses to security threats and symbolised the RMN's continued commitment to defending national sovereignty.
Meanwhile, the statement said the functions and role of PL TSR would be assumed by Auxiliary Vessel Tun Azizan (KA TAZ) to ensure the continuity of the RMN's operational presence and readiness in eastern Sabah.
Originally a mobile offshore production unit known as Hercules 259 built in the United States, the platform was transformed into a forward sea base focused on security through a strategic collaboration involving the RMN, Petronas and Sapura Kencana.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UM can break into world's top 50 universities, says Zambry
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Malaya can break into the world's top 50 universities from its current 58th position, says Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir.
He said while UM's current position was an achievement, the institution was capable of meeting higher targets.
Zambry said that every instance of progress and change in the history of man began with an idea, and that creative and innovative ideas can drive a rise in rankings.
"The empire of the future is the empire of the mind, or in other words, the empire of ideas.
"We can see how a major shift in global education and progress moved from Germany, then to the United States, and is now shifting to China.
"This is due to discoveries and innovations born from top Chinese universities," he said, adding the country was now far ahead in terms of technological advancement.
He said Malaysia had good universities, and that it was not impossible for UM to also rise through the ranks.
"Let us work towards that," he said when officiating the Universiti Malaya Ideas Festival 2026, which brought together over 1,000 researchers and delegates from 90 countries.
Zambry said the event served as an introduction to the national-level Festival of Ideas to be implemented by the Higher Education Ministry.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vietnam's Vingroup forges ahead with 'world's largest stadium' despite demand doubts
Article:
HANOI: Vietnam's Vingroup is accelerating construction in Hanoi of what it says will be the world's largest stadium, betting that future demand will emerge to make the 135,000-seat venue financially viable over the long term.
Thousands of workers are operating around the clock at the site, about 25 kilometres (15.5 miles) south of central Hanoi, to complete the venue by July 2027, a Vingroup representative told Reuters during a site visit this week. The completion date would be a year ahead of the initial plan unveiled in December.
The stadium will have "the world's largest seating capacity" and will also feature the biggest fully retractable roof, said Vingroup, Vietnam's largest firm by market capitalisation, which is simultaneously working on multiple projects, including a high-speed railway, urban developments and wind power plants.
The world's current biggest venue, according to the International Olympic Committee, is the Rungrado Stadium in Pyongyang, North Korea, with a cited capacity of 150,000, although some observers have questioned the figure and counted fewer than 120,000 seats.
Vingroup said the project's scale reflects plans to host large sporting and cultural events, including concerts.
"While football is hugely popular in Vietnam, it is unlikely that a 135,000-seat stadium could be justified on domestic football demand alone," said James Walton, sports business group leader at Deloitte Asia Pacific. He noted Vietnam's top-tier V.League 1 averaged fewer than 6,000 spectators per match in the 2023-24 season.
Vingroup declined to give financial targets, but said it expected the facility to be commercially sustainable long term.
The company faces broader pressures from its financial liabilities, which stood at $36.7 billion last year, accounting for more than 4% of Vietnam's total private debt in 2025. The figure excludes additional debt in private affiliated companies.
DRUM-SHAPED STADIUM
The Trong Dong Stadium, named after the traditional Vietnamese bronze drum, is part of a Vingroup-led $35-billion development of an "Olympic Sports City" designed to host major global events and covering more than 9,000 hectares (22,200 acres) on the outskirts of the capital.
"Being part of a broader urban development can improve the project's long-term financial sustainability," said Walton, who noted many modern national stadiums typically have a 60,000- to 80,000-seat capacity.
The project is part of a massive push to modernise Vietnam's infrastructure and sustain economic growth of at least 10% annually until the end of the decade, which is an explicit goal of the ruling Communist Party.
Authorities have announced hundreds of large-scale projects worth an estimated $200 billion by 2030, including airports, seaports, bridges and railways.
Quynh Nguyen, finance lecturer at Hoa Sen University in Ho Chi Minh City, said modernisation was necessary, but caution was warranted about banks' exposure and funding risks.
"In a growing country like Vietnam, infrastructure often needs to precede demand," said Tran Thi Mong Tuyen, a researcher at the Hawaii-based Pacific Forum, also flagging risks of underused infrastructure and delayed investment returns. (Reporting by Francesco Guarascio; Additional reporting by Phuong Nguyen, Khanh Vu Editing by Ed Davies)
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ministry urges states to raise Muslim marriage age to 18
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Women, Family and Community Development Ministry has urged state governments to raise the minimum age of Muslim marriage to 18.
Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said she hoped other states would follow Selangor and Kedah in raising the minimum marriage age to enable nationwide standardisation.
"Two states have already amended their laws.
"We hope that all states will take similar action so that the matter can be standardised and harmonised nationwide, as Muslim marriage falls under the jurisdiction of the state government," she said.
She noted that although the federal government had discussed the matter at length, the final decision rested with the state authorities.
"We can only voice our concerns, and we even discuss it with the states, but it is not within us to decide," she said.
"It is not under the federal government. So it is up to the state government to realise it," she told reporters after the opening of the Tribunal for Anti-Sexual Harassment (TAGS) office and the launch of the e-TAGS system here today.
Yesterday, the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) said it was pushing for a nationwide minimum marriage age of 18 for Muslims.
It added that a
judicial mechanism
should be retained to allow minors aged 16 and 17 to apply for permission to marry.
The commission said the proposed reform was aimed at safeguarding children's rights, education, and health.
The proposed reform was aimed at safeguarding children's rights, education and health.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM9m in alleged false claims: MACC remands 73 agents, company owners in Daya Kerjaya probe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has remanded 73 individuals, comprising agents and company owners, on suspicion of falsifying Daya Kerjaya 2.0 incentive claims worth about RM9 million.
According to an MACC source, all the suspects, comprising 48 men and 25 women aged between their 20s and 70s, were detained on Tuesday afternoon and night at MACC offices nationwide, except in Perlis.
“Initial investigations found that all the suspects are believed to have committed the acts between 2024 and 2025.
“All the suspects are believed to have submitted false claims involving government wage assistance of RM1,500 per month for six months for each employee hired by private-sector employers and government-linked companies (GLCs),” the source said.
On June 9, 2026, MACC, through its Intelligence Division, Investigation Division and state MACC offices, carried out an integrated operation in cooperation with the Social Security Organisation (Socso).
During the operation, searches were conducted at 90 locations involving company premises, and 83 individuals were detained, comprising six agents and 77 company owners.
Intelligence gathered since November last year had targeted 143 company premises nationwide and 320 individuals linked to the claims.
The source said MACC also recorded 104 statements during the operation, involving 32 persons under investigation and 72 witnesses.
“Through this operation, MACC also froze 25 company bank accounts containing about RM390,000,” the source said.
Meanwhile, MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman, when contacted, confirmed the arrests and said the case is being investigated under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009.
He added that investigations are ongoing and updates will be provided from time to time.

Title: Penang High Court upholds dog‑beating convictions, reduces fines from RM50,000 to RM30,000 each
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 11 — The Penang High Court has upheld the conviction of two men for beating a dog three years ago but reduced the fine imposed on each of them from RM50,000 to RM30,000.
Justice Choong Yeow Choy dismissed Rosli Mat Zin and Suhaili Haron’s appeal against their conviction under Section 29 (1) of the Animal Welfare Act 2015, finding that the conviction was grounded on admissible direct evidence and properly admitted video evidence.
However, he allowed their appeal against the sentence and varied the RM50,000 fine in default 12 months’ imprisonment imposed by the Sessions Court with a fine of RM30,000 each, in default six months’ imprisonment.
In his judgment dated June 6, Justice Choong said a clear and unequivocal message must be conveyed to the public that Section 29(1) of the Animal Welfare Act 2015 should be taken seriously, as the provision serves not only a punitive function but also an important deterrent and educative purpose in the public interest.
“This court is of the view that the appropriate sentence in the present case is a fine. Accordingly, a fine of RM30,000 is imposed on each appellant, and in default of payment, six months’ imprisonment,” he said.
Section 29 (1) of the Act provides that upon conviction, a person is liable to a fine of not less than RM20,000 and not more than RM100,000, or imprisonment for a term not exceeding three years, or both.
According to the judgment, Rosli, 56, a fisherman and Suhaili, 52, a bus driver, were convicted by the Sessions Court for beating a dog after a full trial concluded on July 31, last year.
They were found guilty of beating a dog in Kampung Teluk Tempoyak, Batu Maung, Penang, at 8.30 pm on July 8, 2023.
Justice Choong rejected the appellants’ argument that the trial judge had erred in fact and law by failing to appreciate that the police investigation was conducted contrary to the provisions and procedures of the Animal Welfare Act.
The appellants contended that under that Act, only appointed animal welfare officers have the statutory authority to investigate offences under the Act.
However, Justice Choong held that while the Act contains provisions relating to animal welfare officers, those provisions do not exclude the general investigative powers of the police, which derive from the Criminal Procedure Code and the Police Act 1967.
He said that no provision in the Animal Welfare Act restricted the police from handling cases arising under the Act. — Bernama

Title: Deputy IGP says fentanyl‑based ‘Piu Piu’ leaves users zombie‑like, calls for total vape ban
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — The emergence of “Piu Piu”, a new type of synthetic drug in the market which has been detected being mixed into electronic cigarette liquids, proves that the devices should be completely banned in the country.
Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said the drug, a mixture of fentanyl and psychoactive chemicals, could cause severe intoxication effects, leaving users in a zombie-like state.
He said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) viewed vape usage as having deviated from its original purpose and being widely abused by drug trafficking syndicates as a modus operandi to evade detection by the authorities.
“The main issue at present is not whether vaping presents a business opportunity for certain groups, but that the device is being dangerously misused by mixing it with new drugs or New Psychoactive Substances (NPS).
“Vaping has now become a source of pride among teenagers, so we need to curb this problem at an early stage. Many countries have already banned it, and Malaysia must also have the political will to eradicate it,” he told a press conference here today.
Earlier Ayob Khan officiated a hospital visit programme held in conjunction with the 219th Police Day celebration at Hospital Tunku Ampuan Besar Tuanku Aishah Rohani, the Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia Specialist Children’s Hospital (HPKK UKM), here.
Elaborating further, he said the police did not have the absolute authority to directly ban the use of the devices as the matter also falls under the jurisdiction of other ministries, including the Ministry of Health.
He said PDRM’s Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department would continue carrying out monitoring and large-scale operations on an ongoing basis, including at vape retail outlets, to curb the spread of the latest drug. — Bernama

Title: Ampang English teacher pleads not guilty to charges of sexually assaulting nine‑year‑old pupil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — A male teacher pleaded not guilty at the Ampang Sessions Court to two charges of committing physical sexual assault against a female pupil last month.
Mohd Saufi Jama’in, 45, is accused of committing the acts against a nine-year-old girl, with whom the accused had a relationship of trust as her English teacher.
The offence was allegedly committed at the teacher’s dining area in a school canteen near here at 9am on May 5 and May 7.
He was charged under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, read together with Section 16 of the same Act.
The offence is punishable by a maximum prison sentence of 20 years and caning, while Section 16 carries an additional penalty of up to five years’ imprisonment and no less than two strokes of the cane, upon conviction.
Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Hairuliqram Hairuddin offered bail of RM20,000 with one surety for both charges, along with additional conditions that the accused must report to the nearest police station once a month and refrain from contacting or disturbing the victim and tampering with witnesses.
The accused’s lawyer, Francis Pereira, requested bail of RM4,000 for each charge, citing that his client, who has worked as a teacher for nearly 20 years, supports four children and also suffers from hypertension.
“The accused has no criminal record and has never faced disciplinary action throughout his service. He has fully cooperated with police and poses no flight risk,” he said.
Judge Ho Kwong Chin granted bail of RM12,000 with one surety for both charges, allowed the additional conditions requested by the prosecution, and set August 4 for case mention. — Bernama

Title: Perak police continue probe despite apology over TikTok claims defaming Tok Batin, hiker Jaslinda
Article:
IPOH, June 11 — Police will continue investigating a man who allegedly posted defamatory content about the Tok Batin of Kampung Orang Asli Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, linked to the discovery of hiker Jaslinda Saludin, despite his public apology.
Tapah district police chief Supt Abdul Malik Hasim said investigations will be conducted under Section 500 of the Penal Code and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, following 11 police reports lodged by various parties, including a group of Tok Batin, over the issue.
“We will record the man’s statement soon and obtain official verification from the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) on several matters, including the content of the video,” he said when contacted by Bernama today.
Yesterday, the man issued a public apology via a video recording following controversy over a livestream on his TikTok account, which allegedly defamed Mohd Asmmadi and Jaslinda.
Earlier, during a livestream on the same account, the man allegedly claimed that Jaslinda had been ‘kept’ by Mohd Asmmadi for two weeks, and further alleged that if the matter had not gone viral, the Tok Batin might have married the woman.
Jaslinda, 49, was found safe by villagers in Kampung Lubuk Gaharu on Saturday, after being missing for 14 days since May 24 while hiking Gunung Batu Putih, which stands at approximately 2,132 metres. — Bernama

Title: Rohingya man charged with murder after fellow countryman stabbed to death in Seberang Perai
Article:
NIBONG TEBAL, June 11 — A Rohingya man was charged at the Jawi Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of his fellow countryman in a residential area last month.
Osman Bawdi Alom, 38, nodded to indicate he understood after the charge was read in Malay before Magistrate Nurul Aina Ahmad. However, no plea was recorded as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
He is accused of murdering Abdul Rahman Shah Mia, 31, at Lorong Belibis 14, Taman Belibis, South Seberang Perai, here on May 31.
The charge was framed under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years, as well as not less than 12 strokes of the cane, if the death penalty is not imposed, upon conviction.
The accused also pleaded not guilty to a charge of causing injury to the murder victim’s wife, Formin Lal Mia, 26, with a knife at the same location and time.
For that offence, he was charged under Section 324 of the Penal Code, which provides for a maximum prison sentence of 10 years, or a fine, or caning, or a combination of two punishments, upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Airina Syazreen Zainurin appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was represented by lawyer G. Sathiash.
Nurul Aina then set Aug 7 as the case mention date for submission of the post-mortem report and denied bail for the accused.
Meanwhile, proceedings on charges against two Rohingya women who were also arrested following the murder have been postponed to June 23 for a Myanmar interpreter to read the charges to Humaira, 18, and Minara, 29.
In the incident at around 3.20pm, the victim was pronounced dead at the scene from multiple stab wounds, while his wife also suffered injuries.
Police subsequently arrested a man and two women to assist with investigations. — Bernama

Title: Man, 67, remanded in Batu Pahat over alleged rape of 13-year-old granddaughter
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — A 67-year-old man has reportedly been remanded for six days in Batu Pahat on suspicion of raping his 13-year-old granddaughter last year.
According to
Utusan Malaysia
, Batu Pahat district police chief Assistant Commissioner Shahrulanuar Mushaddat Abdullah Sani confirmed that police had arrested the elderly suspect, who is now under remand.
The remand order was issued by Magistrate Arun Noval Dass to allow police to complete investigations under Section 376(3) of the Penal Code.
Police reportedly received a report two days ago after the victim told her mother about the alleged abuse.
Initial investigations found that the teenager had been living with her grandfather in Batu Pahat after her parents divorced, before her mother brought her to a northern state in April.
Utusan Malaysia
reported that the alleged abuse came to light after the victim’s mother noticed a message sent by the teenager to an acquaintance, with the last incident believed to have taken place in December last year.

Title: Caught with 490 turtle eggs, man gets 18 months’ jail and RM50,000 fine in Sabah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — A foreign man has reportedly been sentenced to 18 months’ jail and fined RM50,000 after pleading guilty to possessing 490 turtle eggs from fully protected species in Sabah.
According to
Sinar Harian
, the accused, Manan Saibung, who had no identification documents, admitted to the charge before Sessions Court Judge Roslinda Razali in Sandakan on Thursday.
He was reportedly found with eggs from the green turtle (Chelonia mydas) and hawksbill turtle (Eretmochelys imbricata) species without valid permission.
The offence was said to have taken place at about 5.50pm on June 7 by the roadside in front of a premises in Sandakan.
Both species are listed as fully protected wildlife under the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997, which provides for a fine of between RM50,000 and RM250,000 and imprisonment of between one and five years upon conviction.
The court also ordered Manan to be handed over to the Immigration Department for further action after he completes his sentence.
According to the report, the prosecution was conducted by Sabah Wildlife Department prosecuting officer Primus Lambut.

Title: Driver found dead after Toyota Innova bursts into flames in early‑morning crash with trailer near Pantai Remis
Article:
IPOH, June 11 — A driver was found dead after the vehicle he was travelling in caught fire following a crash involving a sport utility vehicle (SUV) and a trailer lorry along Jalan Batu 9, Kampung Semangat, Segari, near Pantai Remis early this morning.
Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 4.36am, prompting a team from the Pantai Remis Fire and Rescue Station to be dispatched to the scene.
“Upon arrival at 4.52am, firefighters found a Toyota Innova engulfed in flames, with about 90 per cent of the vehicle destroyed after it was believed to have collided with a Nissan trailer lorry.
“After the fire was extinguished, rescue personnel discovered a victim trapped inside the vehicle. The victim’s identity and gender have yet to be determined,” he said in a statement today.
Sabarodzi said the 60-year-old trailer driver sustained minor injuries and received initial treatment from medics before being taken to hospital by ambulance for further treatment.
According to Sabarodzi, firefighting operations were carried out using a hose reel supplied by the fire engine’s water tank until the blaze was completely extinguished.
“Firefighters also used specialised rescue-cutting equipment to remove the victim’s body before handing it over to the police for further action. The team also carried out clean-up work to remove vehicle debris and clear an oil spill caused by the accident,” he said.
He added that the operation was brought under control at 5.30am and concluded fully at 6.48am. — Bernama

Title: Unemployed man charged with murder after acquaintance found dead in rolled‑up mattress in Melaka
Article:
MELAKA, June 11 — An unemployed man was charged at the Ayer Keroh Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of an acquaintance at a shophouse in March.
Kamil Harris Mohd Abd Nasir, 40, nodded in understanding after the charge was read before Magistrate Uthman Abd Ghani. However, no plea was recorded as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
He is accused of murdering Mohd Idris Yasmir, 40, at Taman Seri Pandan 1, Jalan Pokok Mangga here between 10pm, March 30 and 3am, March 31.
The charge was framed under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which provides for the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years, and if the death penalty is not imposed, shall also be liable to not less than 12 strokes of the cane, upon conviction.
The prosecution was handled by prosecuting officer Insp Nur Zaidah Muhamad Zailani, while the accused was unrepresented. The court set July 29 for the case mention.
It was previously reported that the body of a man, believed to be a murder victim, was discovered wrapped in a rolled-up mattress with his hands tied at a shophouse in the Pokok Mangga area at 7pm on May 28. — Bernama

Title: Students unhappy over haircut, discipline teacher allegedly ends up punched in Kuala Kangsar
Article:
PARIT, June 11 — A discipline teacher was reportedly injured after being punched by a group of students at a secondary school in Kuala Kangsar on Wednesday.
The incident, which occurred around 11.30am, is said to have been triggered when the teacher cut the hair of one of the students involved,
Sinar Harian
(SH) reported.
Perak executive councillor for education, higher learning, youth and sports Datuk Khairuddin Abu Hanipah said the state government is awaiting an official report from the Perak Education Department (JPN).
He noted that he had yet to receive verified information and was still waiting for JPN’s findings. Khairuddin stressed that if the incident did occur, it was inappropriate as teachers are regarded as murabbi — educators who not only teach but also nurture and guide children.
Further checks are being carried out to confirm the authenticity of the case before any formal statement is issued.
Khairuddin added that students must respect their teachers even if certain aspects of the learning process may be disagreeable.
“Adab (Respect and proper conduct) and values must be placed above knowledge. Without adab, it is difficult to produce individuals who are disciplined and visionary,” he told SH.

Title: No more cash counters: EPF moves contributions to apps, banks and self‑service terminals from July 1
Article:
PETALING JAYA, June 11 — The Employees Provident Fund (EPF) will discontinue remittance counters at all branches nationwide from July 1, 2026, shifting contribution payments fully to digital and alternative channels.
The retirement fund said other service counters would continue operating as usual,
The Star
reported.
Members will be able to make payments through the EPF i Akaun mobile application, internet banking, bank agent counters, Self Service Terminals (SST), among other channels, while employers are advised to use the i‑Akaun (Employer) portal.
The EPF stressed that the closure of receipting counters would not affect members’ or employers’ ability to make payments, as multiple alternative channels remain available.

Title: Knife‑wielding couple nabbed after Grab driver’s throat slashed at Kota Tinggi bus terminal
Article:
KOTA TINGGI, June 11 — Police arrested a couple after they were believed to be involved in a knife-wielding robbery of a ride-sharing driver at the Tun Sri Lanang bus terminal here yesterday.
Kota Tinggi police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said the couple, both aged 20, were nabbed in separate locations by policemen from the Criminal Investigation Division (CID) yesterday.
He said the female suspect was nabbed at the Tun Sri Lanang bus terminal here at 11am, followed by the arrest of the male suspect at Dataran Larkin in Johor Bahru at 2.30pm.
“Police also seized a knife, suspected to be the weapon used in the robbery attempt, from the couple,” he said in a statement today.
Yusof was commenting on police investigations into the attempted robbery of the Grab driver who was injured after his throat was slit in the incident.
In preliminary investigations, Yusof said that the suspects tested negative for any narcotic abuse.
However, he said both had prior criminal records.
“The couple was earlier brought to the Magistrate’s Court here this morning for a remand application.
“Police were granted a five-day remand period for the suspects to assist in the investigation,” he said.
